title: Bound V65B terminal artifact lock waits
description: Success/failure publication used blocking flock(LOCK_EX) outside any deadline, so a wedged cooperating publisher could hang terminal resolution indefinitely.
acceptance_criteria: Acquire the directory lock nonblocking with fixed monotonic polling/deadline in runtime and finalizer; timeout is nonzero failure; sealed scope and regression cover contention.
close_reason: terminal_artifact_lock_v65b now uses LOCK_NB with the sealed 300s deadline; builder/finalizer scope and a contention test were updated.

title: Protect V65B preflight and attempt publication from termination
description: SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers were installed only after run claim, GPU preflight and ATTEMPT publication, so default termination could leave a claimed run without a truthful FAILURE.
acceptance_criteria: Install protected handlers before the entire live execution path; early failures after ownership publish a sanitized failure when possible; restore original handlers on every exit.
close_reason: Wrapped the entire live execute path with fixed handlers and idempotent early-failure publication; test proves handler installation precedes guarded work.

title: Make V65B repeating SIGALRM teardown idempotent
description: A pending 100ms repeat alarm could raise while the watchdog finally block was disarming/restoring itself, leaving the alarm or handler installed and disrupting terminal publication.
acceptance_criteria: The handler becomes non-raising before disarm; timer and handler restoration use nested finally semantics; a simulated pending repeat during teardown cannot escape.
close_reason: Added disarming state and nested timer/handler restoration with a pending-repeat regression.

title: Prevent durable V65B report demotion
description: After REPORT was atomically published, SIGTERM or a broken stdout consumer could still enter the failure path and create a contradictory FAILURE, blocking independent finalization.
acceptance_criteria: Signals are suppressed once final durable publication begins; console output is best-effort; failure publication refuses an existing REPORT or FINALIZED; adversarial tests cover closed stdout and terminal exclusivity.
close_reason: Marked cleanup/success ownership before REPORT publication, made JSON console output best-effort, and made failure publication refuse durable report/finalized claims; tests pass.

title: Block evaluation and checkpoints from poisoned ES state
description: After an uncertain actor/update failure marks the trainer poisoned, a caller that catches the exception could still run QA/OOD evaluation or serialize uncertain weights.
acceptance_criteria: QA eval, OOD eval and all checkpoint publication paths reject poisoned trainer state before actor RPC or artifact mutation; regression proves no save RPC occurs.
close_reason: Added a shared healthy-state assertion to QA eval, OOD scoring and atomic checkpoint publication; poisoned save regression passes.

title: Preserve primary trainer failures when cleanup also fails
description: A close_trainer exception raised from finally replaced the original training or OOD gate failure, obscuring the actual cause.
acceptance_criteria: If work and cleanup both fail, re-raise the original exception and attach sanitized cleanup context; cleanup-only failures still propagate.
close_reason: Added close_trainer_preserving_primary using sys.exception; regression proves the original exception identity survives with a cleanup note.

title: Retain partial Ray engine resources during trainer construction
description: launch_engines held placement groups and actors only in local lists until all allocations succeeded. Constructor timeout/failure left trainer.cleanup without handles for partially launched resources.
acceptance_criteria: Attach placement groups and actors to the trainer incrementally before subsequent allocation/waits can fail; partial-construction cleanup can target every known resource.
close_reason: launch_engines now populates self.pgs and self.engines incrementally before readiness/next actor operations.

title: Evaluate every completed ES update at eval_freq cadence
description: The adapter passed a zero-based loop index to eval_step and skipped iteration 0 to avoid overwriting baseline. With eval_freq=1 and three updates, update 1 was never evaluated and update 2 was mislabeled as step 1.
acceptance_criteria: Baseline remains distinct; cadence uses completed update counts; N=3/eval_freq=1 evaluates baseline and post-update states 1,2,3; checkpoint labels match applied updates.
close_reason: Cadence now uses completed_steps=iteration+1 and passes that exact state label; regressions cover eval_freq 1 and 2.

title: Accept permuted Ray actor-to-GPU assignments in V65B
description: The controller bound actor rank N to physical GPU N. Ray guarantees exclusive resource allocation, not actor creation order, so a valid one-to-one permutation such as 2,0,3,1 was rejected.
acceptance_criteria: Accept any exact one-to-one actor/PID mapping over physical GPUs 0..3; keep rank/identity receipt linkage exact; cover a permuted assignment in CPU tests.
close_reason: Removed rank-equals-GPU assumption while retaining exact types and the one-to-one set proof; added a permuted-order binding test.

title: Atomically claim the V65B calibration run
description: Two controllers could both pass the fresh-path check, overwrite the shared attempt artifact, and only then collide while creating RUN_DIR.
acceptance_criteria: Exactly one controller atomically creates RUN_DIR before mutable launch work; a second controller fails without overwriting ATTEMPT; CPU test covers single-owner behavior.
close_reason: RUN_DIR is now an atomic mkdir claim before GPU preflight and before ATTEMPT publication; focused single-owner test passes.

title: Fail closed on partial ES RPC dispatch
description: Perturbation and generation ObjectRef creation occurred before their guarded try blocks. A local dispatch exception after earlier actors accepted RPCs could escape without a restore attempt or poisoned-state marker.
acceptance_criteria: RPC construction is inside the protected ES wave state machine; any partial perturb dispatch poisons the trainer, and any partial generation dispatch still attempts exact restoration before failing.
close_reason: Moved perturbation and generation RPC creation inside guarded loops; partial perturb dispatch now poisons immediately, and partial generation dispatch flows through restoration before the wave aborts.

title: Replace legacy BF16 full-weight ES noise with canonical exact state
description: The versioned upstream full-weight worker resets each parameter-local generator to the same seed and perturbs/restores BF16 weights by additive subtraction. This correlates flattened noise prefixes across parameters, cannot exactly restore BF16 state, and casts small FP32 updates back to BF16 where they can vanish. The current fail-closed adapter refuses reuse after uncertain RPCs but cannot prove exact restoration after a successful wave.
acceptance_criteria: Use a canonical FP32 master or equivalent exact transaction, derive non-overlapping deterministic noise across the full parameter stream, prove bit-identical restoration and meaningful update magnitude on Qwen3.6, and add interruption/partial-RPC regressions.

title: Bound current LoRA generation and actor RPC waits with fail-closed cleanup
description:
## Evidence
The current V52 pipeline and V65 evaluator wait on Ray generation or actor RPCs without a fixed timeout. A wedged actor can hang indefinitely before the exception path restores the pinned master and proves GPU idle. Preserve the current four-actor exact-state protocol and use a preregistered non-adaptive timeout.

## Steps to Reproduce
Replace one generation or collective RPC reference with a test double that never becomes ready. The controller remains in ray.wait or ray.get indefinitely and never reaches its existing restore and GPU-idle cleanup path.

## Scope
Add fixed, recipe-bound timeouts and fail-closed cleanup to current canonical LoRA runs without adaptive retries.
acceptance_criteria: Generation and critical actor RPC waits have fixed recipe-bound timeouts; timeout causes nonzero failure, exact pinned-master restoration when reachable, actor teardown, and four-GPU idle proof; no adaptive retry, row dropping, or reordering occurs; receipts record timeout controls without raw prompts; tests exercise simulated hangs and cleanup failure reporting.
notes:
2026-07-16 V65B portion implemented: fixed 300s actor waits, 250ms monitor/signal polling during generation, bounded constructor/RPC/subprocess/cleanup paths, monitor readiness handshake, strict actor/pool teardown, timeout receipts, and adversarial CPU tests. Kept open until earlier current V52/V65 execution paths are migrated or explicitly superseded.
K3 current-adapter follow-up: added a fixed 300s wrapper around inherited constructor/train/eval Ray gets, explicit bounded perturb/generate/restore/OOD/checkpoint/placement-group waits, poison-on-train-wait failure, and partial-construction-safe cleanup. Still open for earlier V52/V65 paths and external supervision of ray.init itself.
Final local audit confirmed ray.init itself remains outside bounded Ray-get coverage. Do not use a daemon-thread timeout: it can leave startup mutating state in the background. Resolve with an external/process-level startup watchdog or a genuinely bounded Ray startup primitive.

title: Execute exactly num_iterations updates in legacy fit mode
description:
## Evidence
The legacy fit loop increments iteration and stops only when iteration is greater than num_iterations, so it executes one extra ES update and labels the final checkpoint with the requested count. Exact-step mode already uses range(steps) correctly. See es-at-scale/es_at_scale/trainer/es_trainer.py around lines 603-645.

## Steps to Reproduce
Use a finite repeating dataloader, replace train_step with a counter, set num_iterations to 1, and call fit. The legacy loop invokes train_step twice.

## Scope
Correct legacy fit mode while preserving exact-step mode behavior and checkpoint evidence.
acceptance_criteria: For requested counts 0, 1, and N, exactly that many train_step calls occur; checkpoint naming and recorded step counts match applied updates; the final saved state has corresponding evaluation evidence or is explicitly marked ungated; exact-step behavior is unchanged; regression tests cover dataloader wraparound.
close_reason: Replaced inherited N+1 fit loop with exact range(num_iterations), final-state evaluation and atomic checkpoint publication; regression proves exactly three updates for num_iterations=3.

title: Enforce the OOD gate before checkpoint promotion
description:
## Evidence
run_exact_steps writes the final checkpoint before computing compare_ood_prose, records gate failure only in JSON, and returns success. A degraded checkpoint can therefore look promotable. See train_eggroll_es_specialist.py around lines 681-750.

## Steps to Reproduce
Run an exact-step test double with save_final_checkpoint enabled and an OOD comparison forced to fail. Observe that the passing checkpoint path is created before the gate object and the function still returns normally.

## Scope
Reorder and enforce checkpoint promotion without persisting protected OOD content.
acceptance_criteria: The OOD comparison is computed before promotion; a failed gate cannot appear in the passing checkpoint location; summary evidence is always written atomically; failure is machine-visible through nonzero status or an explicit failed-gate artifact; pass and fail regression tests verify ordering and paths; no protected OOD text is persisted.
close_reason: OOD gate is now evaluated and failure summary atomically published before any checkpoint directory is created; passing checkpoints publish by atomic directory rename; regressions cover pass, gate failure and save failure.

title: Make reward timeouts produce consistent zero-valued eval artifacts
description:
## Evidence
In _postprocess_outputs the timeout branch appends zero to aggregate rewards but leaves r and fmt uninitialized or stale. Eval JSON can therefore crash on a first timeout or contain the previous sample reward, and run_summary metrics derived from that JSON can disagree with the aggregate. See es-at-scale/es_at_scale/trainer/es_trainer.py around lines 276-295.

## Steps to Reproduce
Run _postprocess_outputs with eval enabled and a mocked AsyncResult that raises multiprocessing.TimeoutError on the first item; it references unbound r or fmt. Repeat with one successful item before the timeout and observe the prior reward and format copied into the timed-out row.

## Scope
Correct both aggregate and persisted evaluation semantics before changing scorer parallelism.
acceptance_criteria: Every timed-out reward has one explicit timeout format label and numeric reward 0.0 in both aggregates and persisted rows; first-item and later-item timeouts cannot raise or reuse prior state; eval_metrics agrees with _postprocess_outputs aggregates; focused timeout regression tests pass.
close_reason: Fixed timeout rows to initialize as label=timeout/reward=0, validate finite scores and batch cardinality, with regressions covering timeout-before/after-success.

title: Fail closed and exactly restore weights after generation or interruption failures
description:
## Evidence
The legacy evaluate_population_on_batch path perturbs each engine, waits for generation, and restores only on success. Any generation exception can strand a perturbation. The signal handler also exits zero, which can make interrupted runs appear successful. Current V41/V52 LoRA workers use canonical-master restoration, so this issue must harden the legacy path without regressing that newer transaction model. See es-at-scale/es_at_scale/trainer/es_trainer.py around evaluate_population_on_batch and _handle_exit.

## Steps to Reproduce
Inject a Ray generation-resolution exception after perturb_self_weights succeeds, then inspect the engine identity and observe that restore_self_weights was not called. Invoke the signal handler and observe exit status zero.

## Scope
Harden legacy full-weight failure handling and retain the newer canonical LoRA transaction guarantees.
acceptance_criteria: Generation, Ray, scoring, and interruption failure injections cannot leave a lasting perturbation; restoration executes from an exact canonical state in finally-style cleanup; a post-restore identity or checksum is verified; interrupted runs exit nonzero; tests cover failure before generation, during resolution, and during restore; current LoRA state tests remain green.
notes: 2026-07-16 pass: adapter now restores every fully committed perturbation wave after generation failure, poisons state on partial perturb/restore uncertainty, refuses later calls, exits signals nonzero, and bounds cleanup. Kept open because legacy BF16 subtractive restoration cannot satisfy exact canonical-state/checksum acceptance; tracked concretely in specialist-nen.10.

title: Harden and optimize EGGROLL-ES trainer after verified K3 audit
description: Track only findings independently confirmed against the local trainer. Keep legacy full-weight defects distinct from the canonical LoRA path, preserve exact-state and OOD guarantees, and require targeted regression tests plus measured performance evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Every child issue is resolved or explicitly superseded; correctness changes have regression tests; performance changes have before/after measurements on the four-GPU target hardware; canonical LoRA and legacy full-weight scope is documented.
notes:
Three max-reasoning Kimi K3 reviews completed and were independently triaged: trainer raw /tmp/k3_trainer_latest.json SHA-256 5d83684a254ba827cbf42abfcf663e8d7ec899e9f3ea7c946c8a2f66ea648e0f; stats raw /tmp/k3_stats_latest.json (88,556 tokens); final frozen runtime raw /tmp/k3_runtime_latest.json SHA-256 b2a0bdb956cfec742722562b39372e725d2351d0f156700237cc3a039cb49e3f (62,764 tokens, 25,844 reasoning, non-truncated). False runtime claims about partial-pool orphaning and dead wall-clock tolerance were rejected against inherited/finalizer code; credible findings are fixed or tracked in child Beads.
Stats K3 raw SHA-256: 3d7d598df0ddb31741d729fd95e0510a303b3fd1be5aeeeae22c16db6a4a9e49.

title: Prove per-process useful GPU work in every V65B generation phase
description: Current evidence proves the expected compute PID is resident, no foreign compute PID exists, and device-wide utilization is positive. NVML device utilization is not itself attributable to that PID; graphics/display activity could theoretically supply the positive sample.
acceptance_criteria: Add per-process NVML utilization or actor-side CUDA event/work receipts for every generation phase and GPU; bind it into preregistration/evidence/finalizer; preserve device-wide thermal/foreign-process checks; adversarial tests reject resident-but-idle actors.

title: Sanitize V65B uncaught CLI failures
description: Although JSON artifacts hash exception messages, uncaught runtime exceptions could emit raw Ray/vLLM traceback text to durable stderr logs.
acceptance_criteria: The live CLI boundary emits only exception type and message SHA-256, returns nonzero, and never echoes raw exception text; argparse SystemExit behavior remains intact.
close_reason: Added sanitized_cli_v65b with hash-only stderr output and an adversarial raw-text regression.

title: Report V65B actor timeout origin truthfully
description: The failure artifact labeled every TimeoutError—including pool, monitor, cleanup and idle timeouts—as actor_wait_timeout_observed.
acceptance_criteria: Actor/RPC/generation deadlines use a dedicated timeout subtype; generic fixed timeouts still invalidate the run but do not set actor_wait_timeout_observed.
close_reason: Introduced V65BActorWaitTimeout at bounded Ray/generation sites and separated it from generic timeout invalidation.

title: Reject numeric aliases in V65B GPU PID evidence
description: Runtime and finalizer compared expected_pid with == but did not require an exact integer, so a tampered JSON value such as 100.0 passed Python numeric equality.
acceptance_criteria: Both independent validators require type(expected_pid) is int and exact equality to the physical-GPU PID map; adversarial runtime and finalizer tests reject float aliases.
close_reason: Added exact-int checks to both GPU validators and float-alias mutation coverage in both test suites.

title: Cover Ray import with V65B construction watchdog
description: The sealed receipt called the watchdog whole-trainer-construction, but import ray occurred before entering it and could stall without the advertised deadline.
acceptance_criteria: The fixed SIGALRM construction watchdog starts before importing Ray and remains active through inherited trainer construction.
close_reason: Moved import ray inside the fixed whole-construction watchdog; focused V65B tests pass.

title: Publish QA evaluation JSON atomically
description: The adapter still reaches upstream eval_step, which writes each model_eval_task*.json by json.dump(open(final_path, 'w')). A crash or signal during the write leaves a truncated artifact; later eval_metrics or checkpoint selection may read it. Final and OOD summaries already use atomic writers.
acceptance_criteria: QA eval rows are written to a sibling temporary file, fsynced, and atomically renamed; baseline/periodic/final naming and metrics remain identical; failure-injection tests prove the final path is either absent/old or a complete valid JSON array.
close_reason: QA eval now uses atomic_json_write (fsync plus atomic rename); failure injection proves prior artifact survives a rename failure, and focused tests pass.

title: Snapshot or revalidate V65B implementation closure at final publication
description: The finalizer reconstructs the preregistration implementation bindings and records finalizer/test hashes, but implementation files can change between reconstruction and the exclusive finalized-artifact write. Source artifacts are also path-bound rather than retained as an immutable multi-file snapshot.
acceptance_criteria: Final publication derives from immutable byte snapshots or revalidates every source and implementation hash under a publication protocol immediately before the exclusive write; adversarial mutation tests fail closed.

title: Move V65B cleanup deadlines outside catchable in-process alarms
description: The repeating SIGALRM plus elapsed-time rejection catches returned-over-deadline work, but an inner infinite loop that catches BaseException can still prevent cleanup and idle proof from returning. Critical cleanup primitives need external or individually bounded supervision.
acceptance_criteria: No blocking cleanup primitive can outlive its sealed deadline even if called code catches BaseException; timeout must terminate actors/processes, record failure, and preserve a nonzero exit with four-GPU idle proof where physically possible.
notes: K3 found and this pass fixed the in-process repeating-SIGALRM teardown race with disarming state and nested restoration. The issue remains open for its broader acceptance: uninterruptible native/Ray code still needs an external hard watchdog.

title: Shard legacy evaluation across all selected GPU engines
description: Legacy eval_step sends every evaluation batch to engine 0, leaving the other selected GPUs idle. The OOD dispatch helper and current LoRA evaluators already provide order-restoring multi-engine patterns that can be reused carefully.
acceptance_criteria: Evaluation partitions work across every selected engine and restores source order exactly; aggregate metrics and persisted rows match the single-engine reference; uneven batch sizes are handled; per-engine utilization and wall time are recorded on four GPUs; deterministic evaluation outputs do not change beyond documented engine-order effects.
notes:
Max-effort K3 independently confirmed every QA eval batch still uses engines[0], while all four engines are used for training waves and OOD prose. Deterministic sharding needs an equivalence test because changed vLLM batch composition can affect rare greedy ties.
Implemented deterministic round-robin dispatch across all live QA engines with exact source-order restoration, uneven-batch coverage, and cardinality fail-closed tests. Keep open until the acceptance GPU utilization/wall-time and single-engine equivalence evidence is collected on hardware.

title: Preserve seed zero and guarantee unique ES population seeds
description:
## Evidence
Legacy trainer paths use global_seed or 42, silently remapping valid seed 0. Population seeds are sampled with replacement and later stored in dictionaries keyed by seed, so a rare duplicate collapses statistics while remaining duplicated in the update list. Exact-step seed derivation already demonstrates the correct None check.

## Steps to Reproduce
Set global_seed to 0 and inspect train, sampling, and eval seeds; they are based on 42. Force the population RNG to return duplicate integers and observe fewer dictionary entries than update coefficients.

## Scope
Make seed derivation and population identity deterministic and cardinality-preserving in every trainer entry point.
acceptance_criteria: Seed 0 remains 0 in training, sampling, and eval derivations; only None selects the default; every population seed list is unique or rejected before evaluation; aggregation and update inputs have identical cardinality and order; deterministic tests cover seed 0 and forced duplicate draws.
close_reason: Seed zero is preserved through inherited truthiness logic and population seeds are drawn uniquely with deterministic order-preserving rejection sampling; validation occurs before actor RPCs and regressions cover both.

title: Parallelize reward scoring with killable timeout semantics
description: The legacy eight-process pool submits one reward and immediately waits, so only one task is in flight. A timed-out multiprocessing task is not killed, can occupy a worker indefinitely, and close plus join can hang shutdown. The current V52 CPU scoring actors already overlap scoring with subsequent GPU generation and should not be regressed.
acceptance_criteria: A minibatch has bounded concurrent scoring; a timed-out or hung scorer is terminated or replaced; shutdown is bounded; output order and deterministic reward mapping are preserved; tests cover success, exception, timeout, and shutdown; a 200-prompt before/after benchmark reports wall time and throughput; current V52 overlap tests remain green.
notes: Max-effort K3 independently confirmed the current scorer submits then waits one row at a time and timed-out multiprocessing jobs cannot be cancelled; total latency can grow rows × timeout and stuck jobs consume the 8-worker pool. Requires a measured batch/global-deadline redesign.

title: Benchmark eager mode, static-wave scheduling, and redundant CUDA synchronization
description: Credible optimization candidates remain benchmark-dependent: enforce_eager=True disables CUDA graphs, static legacy waves amplify stragglers and leave a partial final wave, driver torch.cuda.synchronize has no matching driver work, and frequent empty_cache calls may add overhead. Do not change sealed deterministic controls from intuition alone.
acceptance_criteria: Run preregistered A/B microbenchmarks for eager versus graph mode, static versus queued wave scheduling, driver synchronization removal, and cache-clearing cadence; report throughput, latency, GPU utilization, memory, output identity, and thermal throttling; adopt only changes that preserve correctness and determinism and show a repeatable benefit.
